content.full_text:
COMPUTATION OF REPORTING THRESHOLDS

For the purpose of computing the $250 income reporting threshold, gifts and other types of income are lumped. Thus, a $25 gift and $225 of other income satisfy the $250 income reporting threshold. Since the gift portion is $25 or more, it also should be itemized on the gift schedule. Thus, if the gift were $20 and the income were $230, the $250 threshold would be met but there would be no itemization on the gift schedule.

If a filer reports $230 of honoraria on the honoraria schedule and $20 of gift, the gift must be itemized on the gift schedule. This is because the honoraria is viewed as a gift for threshold purposes. Thus, since there are more than $25 in gifts being reported (the $230 honoraria) all gifts must be itemized.

If the filer receives $25 in gifts and $50 in income the gifts must be reported on the gift schedule. However, the $50 in income need not be reported since the $50 of income and $25 of gifts equal $75 which is short of the $250 income reporting threshold.
